name: Lake Taupō
notes: A vast volcanic caldera lake famous for hard-fighting wild rainbow and brown trout. Work the river mouths and drop-offs at dawn and dusk.

species.name: Rainbow Trout
species.behavior: Acrobatic fighter that holds near cool inflows.
species.realInfo: Taupō rainbows average ~1.5kg; smelt-imitating flies and harling work well.

species.name: Brown Trout
species.realInfo: Browns here go nocturnal in summer; cicada patterns shine in Feb.

species.name: Common Bully
species.behavior: Tiny native baitfish trout gorge on.

species.name: Koura
species.behavior: Native freshwater crayfish scuttling the bottom.

strategy: Fish river mouths at dawn and dusk when trout move in to feed.
Find the drop-off where shallow shelf meets deep water — browns patrol the edge.
In summer, go early or late; midday sun pushes fish deep.
